response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly one hour daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: Listen, I have been working with pipes and drains for more years than most people have been alive, and I can tell you that fake content on social media is like a clogged drain that backs up everything. You cannot just ignore it because it will cause problems eventually. I spend about an hour each day on social media, mostly looking at cooking videos and keeping up with football news from back home, and I see this nonsense everywhere. People sharing things without checking if they are true, spreading rumors like wildfire. It makes me very worried because you never know what to believe anymore. When I came to Philadelphia many years ago, we did not have these problems. You heard news from people you trusted, face to face. Now everyone is an expert on everything, posting whatever they want without any responsibility. My approach is simple and strict. I do not share anything unless I am absolutely certain it is real. If something seems too shocking or too perfect, I do not touch it. I have learned to look at who is posting the information. Is it someone reliable? Is it from a proper news organization? Or is it just some random account trying to get attention? I also check the dates on things because people love to recirculate old stories and pretend they just happened. What really bothers me is when people use fake content to attack others or spread hatred. In Islam, we are taught to verify information before spreading it, and this wisdom applies perfectly to social media. The Quran teaches us not to follow what we have no knowledge of, and that is exactly what people do when they share fake posts. I tell people to slow down, think carefully, and ask themselves if what they are seeing makes sense. Just like in plumbing, you need to check the source of the problem before you start working on it. Do not just believe everything that flows through your feed. Take your time, be careful, and do not contribute to the mess. It is hard work, but somebody has to maintain standards in this world.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The persona is an 82-year-old immigrant woman with high conscientiousness, low openness, low agreeableness, and anxious tendencies. She has no formal education but decades of practical work experience as a plumber. Her tone should be direct, practical, somewhat skeptical, and grounded in her life experience. She would approach social media fake content with the same methodical, careful approach she uses in her work. Her Islamic faith provides moral framework. Her anxiety would make her particularly concerned about misinformation. Her low agreeableness suggests she would be blunt and critical rather than diplomatic. Her high conscientiousness means she would emphasize careful verification and responsibility. Her immigrant background and age give her a perspective comparing past and present communication methods. Limited social media time (one hour daily) means focused, practical usage rather than deep platform knowledge.
response_parsed.tone: Direct, practical, skeptical, blunt, morally grounded, slightly anxious, and drawing from life experience
